frameworks.name: meta-learning with memory
frameworks.one_line: training memory-equipped agents across task distributions to induce implicit inference

frameworks.name: Bayes-optimal agents
frameworks.one_line: risk-neutral, ambiguity-neutral baseline emerging as the implicit meta-training solution

frameworks.name: risk-sensitive decision making
frameworks.one_line: agents that exploit higher-order moments of return distributions

frameworks.name: ambiguity-sensitive decision making
frameworks.one_line: agents that act differently when recognizing situations of unknown uncertainty

core_question: How can the meta-learning protocol be extended so that trained agents become risk- and ambiguity-sensitive rather than Bayes-optimal?
key_assumptions: meta-training with memory implicitly solves a numerical optimization problem
Bayes-optimality is risk- and ambiguity-neutral by construction
human deviations from Bayes-optimality can be advantageous, especially in safety-critical settings
manipulations: modified meta-training algorithms that manipulate the learner's experience-generation process
key_findings: risk- and ambiguity-sensitivity emerge as solutions to modified meta-training optimizations
interpretations: sensitivity to higher-order return moments and to unknown uncertainty can be induced rather than hand-designed
such inductive biases may confer advantages in safety-critical decision making
